---
pagetitle: "Crazyfile Quadrotors in Complex Envrionment"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    page-layout: full
---

::: {.hero-section}


# Crazyfile Quadrotors in Complex Envrionment {.title}

::: {.subtitle}
Implementing a robust ROS2-based controller for agile obstacle avoidance and fast speed.
:::

::: {.author-list}

[**Xiyuan Zhou**](https://github.com/Annaz2024-ECE)^1^,
[**Wenjuan Lin**](https://github.com/LatentLin2512)^1^,
[**Susheendhar Vijay**](https://github.com/psushi)^1^

:::

::: {.affiliation-list}

^1^University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign (UIUC)

:::

::: {.button-row}

[[{{< fa file-pdf >}} Paper]{.btn-text}](https://arxiv.org/pdf/XXXX.XXXXX){.btn .btn-primary}
[[{{< fa brands arxiv >}} arXiv]{.btn-text}](https://arxiv.org/abs/XXXX.XXXXX){.btn .btn-primary}
[[{{< fa video >}} Video]{.btn-text}](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cSQTZoZPJzs){.btn .btn-primary}
[[{{< fa brands github >}} Code]{.btn-text}](https://github.com/){.btn .btn-primary}
[[{{< fa database >}} Data]{.btn-text}](https://example.com){.btn .btn-primary}

:::

:::


<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- TEASER IMAGE / VIDEO -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

::: {.section-container}

::: {.hero-teaser}

<!-- Option A: Use a static image as the teaser -->
![Teaser figure showing your method overview or key result.](static/images/placeholder-teaser.svg){.teaser-img}

<!-- Option B: Embed a video teaser (uncomment below, comment out image above)
{{< video static/videos/teaser.mp4 >}}
-->

:::

:::


<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- ABSTRACT -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

::: {.section-container}

## Abstract {.section-title}

::: {.abstract-text}
This project addresses the challenge of autonomous high-speed navigation 
for micro-quadrotors in constrained environments. Utilizing the Crazyflie 
2.1 platform within a ROS2-based architecture, we developed an integrated 
control and perception pipeline designed to navigate complex, gate-based 
obstacle courses.

Our key contribution lies in the implementation of a robust trajectory 
tracking controller that bridges the gap between high-fidelity Gazebo 
simulations (CrazySim) and physical hardware deployment. To overcome the 
inherent instability of small-scale drones at high velocities, we leveraged 
state estimation tuning and aggressive attitude control strategies.

Experimental results demonstrate that our system achieves collision-free 
autonomous flight across diverse track configurations. Our optimized 
controller reduced lap times by [X]% (e.g., 15%) compared to the baseline 
PID implementation while maintaining a high safety margin. We successfully 
validated the sim-to-real transferability of our algorithms, transitioning 
from zero-collision simulated runs to consistent performance in the physical 
flight arena.
:::

:::


<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- OVERVIEW / METHOD VIDEO -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

::: {.section-container}

## Video {.section-title}

::: {.video-container}
<!-- Replace with your YouTube or local video embed -->
{{< video https://www.youtube.com/embed/cSQTZoZPJzs >}}
:::

:::


<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- RESULTS GALLERY -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

::: {.section-container}

## Results {.section-title}

::: {.content-text}
Provide a brief description of the results shown below. Explain what the
reader should observe and why it matters.
:::

::: {.results-grid}

::: {.result-card}
![Result 1 caption](static/images/placeholder-result1.svg)
:::

::: {.result-card}
![Result 2 caption](static/images/placeholder-result2.svg)
:::

::: {.result-card}
![Result 3 caption](static/images/placeholder-result3.svg)
:::

:::

:::


<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- QUALITATIVE COMPARISONS -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

::: {.section-container}

## Qualitative Comparisons {.section-title}

::: {.content-text}
Describe the comparison setup — which baselines are you comparing against, and
what should the reader look for in the side-by-side results.
:::

::: {.comparison-grid}

::: {.comparison-item}
![Ours](static/images/placeholder-ours.svg)

**Ours**
:::

::: {.comparison-item}
![Baseline A](static/images/placeholder-baseline.svg)

**Baseline A**
:::

:::

:::


<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- INTERACTIVE SLIDER (Optional) -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

::: {.section-container}

## Interpolation Demo {.section-title}

::: {.content-text}
If your method supports interpolation or continuous control, you can add an
interactive slider here. The example below shows how to set one up.
:::

::: {.interpolation-panel}

::: {.interpolation-endpoints}
![Start](static/images/placeholder-start.svg){.endpoint-img}

![End](static/images/placeholder-end.svg){.endpoint-img}
:::

<input type="range" min="0" max="100" value="50" class="interpolation-slider" id="interpolation-slider">
<div id="interpolation-value" class="interpolation-value">50%</div>

<script>
  const slider = document.getElementById('interpolation-slider');
  const display = document.getElementById('interpolation-value');
  slider.addEventListener('input', function() {
    display.textContent = this.value + '%';
  });
</script>

:::

:::


<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- RELATED WORK -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

::: {.section-container}

## Related Work {.section-title}

::: {.content-text}

Here are some related works in this area:

- [D4RT: Teaching AI to see the world in four dimensions](https://deepmind.google/blog/d4rt-teaching-ai-to-see-the-world-in-four-dimensions/) 
introduces a unified 4D reconstruction and tracking framework. 
While our project focuses on ROS2-based control, 
D4RT represents the cutting edge of spatial perception, 
which is essential for high-speed autonomous navigation and "world model" understanding.
- [Related Paper 2](https://example.com) also addresses [problem] using [approach].
- [Related Paper 3](https://example.com) proposes [technique] which is complementary to our method.

Check out [this survey](https://example.com) for a comprehensive overview of the field.
:::

:::


<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- BIBTEX -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

::: {.section-container}

## BibTeX {.section-title}

```bibtex
@article{WayNo2.0-2026project,
  author    = {Zhou Xiyuan and Lin Wenjuan and Vijay Susheendhar},
  title     = {Crazyfile Quadrotors in Complex Environment},
  howpublished = {Project Report for ECE 484: Principles of Safe Autonomy, University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign},
  year      = {2026},
  note      = {Course Project}
}
```

:::


<!-- ============================================================ -->
<!-- FOOTER -->
<!-- ============================================================ -->

::: {.site-footer}

This website template is adapted from the
[Nerfies](https://nerfies.github.io) project page, which is licensed under a
[Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International License](http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/).

:::
